summary: Lisa Park and Rachel Torres from Aegis held a renewal check-in with Philip Lau from Keystone Health. Philip expressed strong satisfaction with Aegis Comply and Protect, citing approximately 40-50 hours saved per quarter on audit prep and solid backup recovery performance. The team discussed the upcoming Aegis Comply v2 launch featuring on-demand multi-framework compliance reporting, which Philip found genuinely exciting given his team's simultaneous HIPAA and SOC 2 Type II obligations. The call concluded positively with agreement to proceed on the core renewal at existing rates with a three-year lock, and to schedule a separate technical demo of the Aegis Detect module with Philip's CISO before making an add-on decision.
actionItems: Lisa Park: Send renewal paperwork for Protect and Comply at current rates by end of week
Lisa Park: Coordinate with solutions engineering team to schedule a Detect module technical demo within 2-3 weeks, including Philip's CISO
Philip Lau: Connect with Lisa about developing a case study on Aegis Comply time savings
Rachel Torres: Follow up on Detect bundle pricing proposal after the demo is completed

keyMoments.text: Philip shares that Aegis Comply has saved his team 40-50 hours per quarter on audit prep and his IT director wants to do a case study
keyMoments.speaker: Philip Lau

keyMoments.text: Rachel announces Aegis Comply v2 launching in early April with on-demand multi-framework compliance reporting included at no extra cost for existing customers
keyMoments.speaker: Rachel Torres

keyMoments.text: Lisa transparently discloses a recent Detect event ingestion pipeline failure that affected monitoring visibility for some customers
keyMoments.speaker: Lisa Park

keyMoments.text: Philip agrees to lock in core renewal and schedule a separate Detect demo with his CISO before deciding on the add-on bundle
keyMoments.speaker: Philip Lau
